prompt: I have an existing 'orders' service using hexagonal architecture (Express HTTP, Postgres write, MongoDB read, Redis cache, Kafka). I need to add a Kafka consumer that listens to events from the payments service. When a 'payment.completed' event arrives, it should mark the corresponding order as 'paid' — add a 'paid' status to the Order entity and a markAsPaid() transition method. When a 'payment.failed' event arrives, it should cancel the order (already exists as CancelOrderUseCase). Add the consumer adapter, its bootstrap, wire it into main.ts, and create the MarkOrderAsPaidUseCase. Do not touch the existing HTTP adapter.

assertions.name: paid-status-on-entity
assertions.description: Order entity adds 'paid' status and markAsPaid() method

assertions.name: consumer-delegates-not-implements-logic
assertions.description: Consumer only parses and calls use case execute()

assertions.name: consumer-skip-on-error
assertions.description: Consumer try/catches per-message, logs, does not throw

assertions.name: consumer-bootstrap-is-separate
assertions.description: Separate bootstrap.ts for the Kafka consumer

assertions.name: main-ts-runs-both
assertions.description: main.ts calls both HTTP and consumer bootstraps

assertions.name: http-adapter-untouched
assertions.description: Existing Express controller and bootstrap are not modified
